message:
Harden numeric accumulators against overflow and precision loss

Replaces silent failure modes across core numeric paths with explicit
guards so a single corrupted counter can no longer masquerade as healthy
progress.

- fcp-audit: swap saturating_add for checked_add when computing the next
  expected chain sequence number.  saturating_add silently accepts a
  chain that stalls at u64::MAX, effectively letting a malicious or
  buggy signer replay the tail indefinitely.  We now emit a new
  audit.seq_overflow VerifyIssue and stop further sequence validation,
  matching the semantics of AuditEntry::follows().  Adds regression test
  verify_chain_seq_overflow_stops_validation.
- fcp-core: round() the u8 success-rate cast in LifecycleRecord.  The
  bare \`as u8\` truncates, so 49.9 -> 49; with round() we preserve the
  intent of a percent readout without ever exceeding 100 (the outer
  .min(100.0) still clamps).
- fcp-raptorq: drop the f64-based K' computation in Decoder::needed().
  For large K values f64 loses integer precision, so the ceil() could
  report a smaller K' than the actual protocol constant.  Replaced with
  integer ceil-division \`(K*2 + 999) / 1000\` and a try_from saturating
  to u32::MAX.  Also make Encoder::total_symbols() saturating so the
  source + repair tally cannot silently wrap.
- fcp-store: use saturating_add for SymbolDistribution totals so a
  misbehaving node cannot wrap total_symbols back to 0 and defeat the
  coverage metrics.
- fcp-streaming: use saturating_add for ReconnectHandler::attempts so
  the exponential-backoff branch cannot wrap to zero and immediately
  reconnect after u32::MAX failures.
